note: Routing / voice-trigger prose extracted from SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions during catalog trim. Loaded on demand when routing guidance is needed.

accessibility-audit.lead: [Accessibility AI] WCAG 2.2 AA conformance audit augmented with assistive-tech behavioral testing. Runs VoiceOver / TalkBack / NVDA / JAWS
accessibility-audit.routing:
via /browse
automation, validates keyboard-only flows, checks colour contrast and
cognitive load, and walks personas (blind, low-vision, deaf,
motor-impaired, cognitive) through real workflows on the live site. Outputs
a conformance level AND an assistive-tech experience score — a site can
pass WCAG and still be unusable for AT users, and this skill catches both.
Use when asked to "accessibility audit", "WCAG audit", "a11y", "screen
reader test", "is this accessible?", or "assistive tech review".

adr.lead: [CTO Strategy] Architecture Decision Record generator + linter (Michael Nygard format).
adr.routing:
Captures architectural decisions as numbered, immutable ADRs so the team remembers WHY a
choice was made six months later. Lints the existing ADR set for stale Status fields,
conflicting decisions, missing context, and broken supersession chains. Output goes to
`docs/adr/{NNNN}-{slug}.md`. Use when: "write an ADR", "document this decision", "ADR
lint", "architecture decision record", "why did we choose X".
adr.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "write an adr", "architecture decision record", "adr lint".

agent-design.lead: [AI Engineering / Research] Designs an LLM agent as a typed state machine, not an ad-hoc while-loop.
agent-design.routing:
Pick a pattern (ReAct / Plan-Solve / Reflexion / Anthropic-style
sub-agents), declare tools, prompts, stopping criteria, and an eval plan.
Also audits an existing agent for infinite loops, hallucinated tools, lost
intent. Outputs agents/{slug}/{state-machine.ts,tools.ts,prompts/,EVAL.md}.
Use when: "design an agent", "build a tool-using LLM", "audit my agent loop",
"why does the agent keep failing".
agent-design.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "agent design", "design an agent", "audit my agent".

agent-ux-review.lead: [Cognitive Computing / HMC] Reviews an AI feature's interaction flow against agent-UX principles — predictability, repair, attention budget, surfaced uncertainty, escape hatches, mode visibility.
agent-ux-review.routing:
Rates each principle 0-10 with concrete examples and edits
the plan or component spec to close gaps. Complement to /plan-ux-review, which covers
non-AI workflows. Surfaces tradeoffs (e.g., predictability vs. usefulness, repair
affordances vs. attention budget) rather than enforcing one style.
Use when asked to "review the agent UX", "AI feature UX review", "agent interaction
review", "is this AI feature usable", or "trust UX for this AI feature".

api-contract-test.lead: [API/DB] Generate consumer-driven contract tests (Pact, or equivalent) from an OpenAPI / GraphQL / Protobuf spec, wire them into CI for both producer and consumer sides, and maintain a...
api-contract-test.routing:
Catches the
"frontend ships expecting one shape, backend ships another" class of bug before
integration. Use when: "contract tests", "pact tests", "consumer driven contracts",
"api compatibility matrix", or "did the API break".

api-design.lead: [API/DB] Design or audit an API surface.
api-design.routing:
Picks REST / GraphQL / gRPC based on use case
and consumer profile, generates OpenAPI 3.1 / GraphQL SDL / Protocol Buffers, and
validates against a chosen style guide (Stripe API Design Guide is the default).
Reviewer mode walks an existing surface, catches breaking changes, scores consistency,
and produces a remediation plan. Use when: "design this API", "audit our API",
"REST vs GraphQL", "generate OpenAPI", "API style guide", or "is this a breaking
change".

aso.routing:
Reads the current App Store / Play Store listing,
analyzes the top-10 competitor listings in the same category, and proposes updates to
title, subtitle, keywords, promo text, and screenshots. Generates updated screenshot
variants via /app-store-screenshots. Writes a monthly proposal under mobile/aso/ with
a clean diff against the current live listing. Use when: "ASO", "app store
optimization", "improve our store listing", "keyword research", "competitor store
listings", or "refresh screenshots".

auto-feature-build.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Single-persona feature builder for gstack.
auto-feature-build.routing:
Auto-discovers MISSING workflows for one persona,
plans and builds each feature in dependency order, verifies as the persona, and
runs autoaudit + auto-ux-audit at the end. Orchestrates office-hours, autoplan,
autobuild, verify-loop, autoaudit, and auto-ux-audit on a queue of features.
Mirrors /auto-ux-audit on the build side: /auto-ux-audit fixes friction in
existing workflows, /auto-feature-build builds the workflows that don't exist yet.
Use when asked to "build missing features", "auto feature build", "build what the
persona needs", "kickstart features for this persona", or "feature gap fill".
For a single user-specified feature, use /feature-build. For all personas at once,
use /auto-feature-build-full.

auto-feature-build-full.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Full feature gap-fill across ALL personas for gstack.
auto-feature-build-full.routing:
Runs gap analysis per persona,
dedups into a unified feature queue, builds each feature in dependency
order, and re-verifies critical workflows for EVERY persona after each
build (not just the feature's primary persona). Cross-persona regression
detection prevents fixes for one persona breaking another's flows.
Orchestrates office-hours, autoplan, autobuild, verify-loop, autoaudit,
and auto-ux-audit-full at the end. Tier 3 of the feature-build family.
Use when asked to "build all missing features", "feature build full",
"auto feature build full", "kickstart for every persona", or
"complete feature gap fill". For single-persona, use /auto-feature-build.
For one feature, use /feature-build.

auto-guard.lead: [Tools] Automatic security + QA hooks for git events.
auto-guard.routing:
Installs Claude Code
hooks that run a lightweight /cso scan on staged files before each commit
(OWASP Top 10, offline, <5s) and an async /qa-only smoke test after each
push. Critical secrets block the commit by default; everything else warns.
Use when asked to "install auto-guard", "auto security check", "pre-commit
scan", "qa on push", or "automate security hooks".
auto-guard.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "auto guard", "install hooks", "pre commit security".

auto-ux-audit.lead: [UX Audit] Single-persona UX audit with auto-fix.
auto-ux-audit.routing:
Runs /ux-audit for one specified persona,
then fixes all friction points, dead ends, and UX gaps found in the source code.
Each fix gets an atomic commit and browser re-verification. Use when asked to
"fix the UX for admins", "auto ux audit", "audit and fix persona workflow",
or "fix the new user experience". For report-only mode, use /ux-audit.
For all personas at once, use /auto-ux-audit-full.

auto-ux-audit-full.lead: [UX Audit] Full UX audit across all personas with auto-fix.
auto-ux-audit-full.routing:
Discovers or accepts personas,
runs workflow simulations for each one on the live app, then iteratively fixes
all friction points in source code. Orchestrates multiple /auto-ux-audit passes
with cross-persona consistency checks between rounds. Use when asked to
"full ux audit", "audit all user types", "fix ux for everyone",
"auto ux audit full", or "complete workflow audit with fixes".
For a single persona, use /auto-ux-audit. For report-only, use /ux-audit.

autoaudit.lead: [Code Review] Post-build verification pipeline for gstack — runs security audit (/cso) then code review (/review + /codex) sequentially.
autoaudit.routing:
Reports findings, auto-fixes obvious issues,
flags ambiguous ones. No shipping — just verification.
Use when asked to "audit", "autoaudit", "verify the build", "security + review",
"check my code", or "is this ready to ship?".
Proactively suggest after the user finishes building from a plan, or after /autobuild.

autobuild.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Autonomous implementation for gstack from an approved plan.
autobuild.routing:
Reads the plan file, enters plan mode,
and builds everything with full autonomy. Clears conversation context to maximize
available tokens for implementation. Designed to run headless or with minimal supervision.
Use when asked to "autobuild", "build the plan", "implement it", "go build",
"execute the plan", or "start building".
Proactively suggest after /autoplan-full or /autoplan produces an approved plan.

autoplan.lead: [Code Review] Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles.
autoplan.routing:
Surfaces
taste decisions (close approaches, borderline scope, codex disagreements) at a final
approval gate. One command, fully reviewed plan out.
Use when asked to "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews", "review this plan
automatically", or "make the decisions for me".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan file and wants to run the full review
gauntlet without answering 15-30 intermediate questions.

autoplan-full.lead: [Code Review] Full CTO planning pipeline for gstack — from raw idea to fully reviewed implementation plan.
autoplan-full.routing:
Runs office-hours → discover → CEO review → UX review → DX review → design review → eng review
sequentially. UX always runs; DX (developer experience) runs when the plan has API/CLI/SDK/
platform/docs surface; design runs when the plan has UI scope. Interactive in phases 1-2
(framing + research), auto-decided in phases 3-6 using decision principles. Two gates:
framing confirmation and final approval.
Use when asked to "full plan", "autoplan-full", "plan everything", "plan from scratch",
"run the full pipeline", or "idea to plan".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a new product idea or feature from scratch.

avatar-design.routing:
Given a use case
(real-time conversational / pre-rendered cinematic / sign-language performer
/ scripted explainer), picks the face model (NeRF / Gaussian Splatting / 3DMM
/ 2D portrait), body model (SMPL-X / VR avatar / 2D), animation driver,
expression vocabulary, voice (from Family L), and integration layer. Outputs
build-ready ARCHITECTURE + SUBSYSTEMS + INTEGRATION + EVAL-HUMANNESS docs
the downstream avatar skills consume.
Use when: "design an avatar", "avatar architecture", "embodied agent stack",
"build a virtual human", "talking-head system", or "sign-performing avatar".
avatar-design.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "design an avatar", "avatar architecture", "build a virtual human", "embodied agent".

avatar-sign.routing:
Ingests gloss
(via /sign-text) or text, maps to sign parameters (via /sign-linguistics) —
handshape + location + movement + non-manual features + prosody — and
animates the avatar with linguistic fidelity, not keyframe stiffness. Native-
deaf-reviewer loop is built in. Bridges Family P (Avatar) + Family L (Speech) +
Family O (Accessibility) + Family G (CV). Outputs PARAM-MAPPER + ANIMATION-
PIPELINE + FLUENCY-EVAL docs and a rendered video.
Use when: "sign-performing avatar", "sign-language avatar", "TSL/ASL avatar",
"gloss-to-animation", or "deaf-accessible avatar".
avatar-sign.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "sign performing avatar", "sign language avatar", "tsl avatar", "asl avatar", "gloss to animation".

backtest.lead: [FinTech / Algorithmic Trading] Point-in-time, look-ahead-free backtest harness for algorithmic strategies.
backtest.routing:
Enforces survivorship-corrected universes, realistic transaction
costs (commissions + slippage + market impact + borrow), realistic fills (LOB-aware or
queue-position model), walk-forward instead of in-sample / out-of-sample, and regime
breakdowns. Emits a backtest report a reviewer can replicate or attack — not a
marketing chart.
Use when: "backtest this strategy", "run a backtest", "walk-forward", "point-in-time
backtest", "look-ahead check".
backtest.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "backtest this strategy", "walk forward", "point in time backtest".

behavioral-experiment.lead: [Operations Research / Behavioral] Design a behavioral A/B / multi-arm experiment with proper power analysis, randomization strategy, primary + guardrail metrics, **pre- registered analysis plan**...
behavioral-experiment.routing:
Cites the underlying behavioral principle being tested (anchoring,
default effects, framing, salience, loss aversion, social proof) and predicts the
expected effect size before the experiment runs.
Use when: "design an experiment", "A/B test", "behavioral experiment", "power
analysis", "pre-registration", "anti p-hacking experiment design".

benchmark.lead: [Ship & Release] Performance regression detection using the browse daemon.
benchmark.routing:
Establishes
baselines for page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes.
Compares before/after on every PR. Tracks performance trends over time.
Use when: "performance", "benchmark", "page speed", "lighthouse", "web vitals",
"bundle size", "load time".

benchmark-models.lead: [Ship & Release] Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills.
benchmark-models.routing:
Runs the same prompt through Claude,
GPT (via Codex CLI), and Gemini side-by-side — compares latency, tokens, cost,
and optionally quality via LLM judge. Answers "which model is actually best
for this skill?" with data instead of vibes. Separate from /benchmark, which
measures web page performance. Use when: "benchmark models", "compare models",
"which model is best for X", "cross-model comparison", "model shootout".
benchmark-models.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "compare models", "model shootout", "which model is best".

biases-audit.routing:
Reviews a decision
document, UX surface, pricing page, hiring loop, or self-decision plan for
target-specific cognitive biases — anchoring, decoy, halo, sunk-cost, confirmation,
availability, narrative fallacy, in-group, framing, default, status-quo, planning
fallacy. This is the DEFENSIVE twin to marketing-psychology — same primitives, but
used to **detect and remove** manipulation, not deploy it. Produces a per-subject
bias map with severity, evidence, and remediation.
Use when: "bias audit", "cognitive biases", "check for dark patterns", "audit this
decision", "check my reasoning", "audit the pricing page", "audit the hiring rubric".

blueprint.lead: [Product Transposition] Structured study of an external target (product, paper, codebase, app, or SDK/API) that produces a domain-agnostic BLUEPRINT.md plus
blueprint.routing:
evidence, notes, and citations.
Five input modes (`--mode product|paper|code|app|api`) each use the right extractor.
Refuses auth-walled targets without ownership declaration. Never copies verbatim
text, code, images, or branding — transformative analysis only. First step of Family J's
constructive reverse-engineering pipeline (`/blueprint → /transpose → /clone-and-twist → /recipe`).
Use when asked to "study this product", "blueprint this paper", "extract the mechanism",
"RE this codebase", "study fingerspelling.xyz", or "blueprint supertonic-v3".

board-deck.lead: [CEO Cockpit] Board deck generator following the YC-style template — Traction, Financials, Product, Team, Risks, Asks.
board-deck.routing:
Each slide pulls from a named data source (Stripe, GitHub,
Linear, HubSpot, prior retros). Hall reviews and edits the narrative, not the data.
Outputs markdown + PPTX + PDF. Compresses 2-3 days of pre-board prep into ~2 hours.
Use when: "board deck", "board meeting prep", "draft the board deck".
board-deck.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "board deck", "board prep", "draft the deck".

browse.lead: [Browser & Web] Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding.
browse.routing:
Navigate any URL, interact with
elements, verify page state, diff before/after actions, take annotated screenshots, check
responsive layouts, test forms and uploads, handle dialogs, and assert element states.
~100ms per command. Use when you need to test a feature, verify a deployment, dogfood a
user flow, or file a bug with evidence. Use when asked to "open in browser", "test the
site", "take a screenshot", or "dogfood this".

bug-bounty-triage.lead: [Red Team / RE] Bug-bounty report triage pipeline.
bug-bounty-triage.routing:
Reads a triaged report (HackerOne /
Bugcrowd / direct), validates reproduction in an isolated sandbox, scores severity
using CVSS v4.0, dedups against intel/iocs.jsonl + prior reports, drafts the internal
fix ticket AND the reply-to-reporter. Two-sided posture: rigorous on impact, generous
on attribution and bounty pay. Refuses to test in production unless the report's program
explicitly allows it.
Use when: "bug bounty triage", "validate report", "cvss score", "h1 report", "bugcrowd",
"dedup vulnerability", "reporter reply", "fix ticket draft", "security report intake".
bug-bounty-triage.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "triage bug bounty", "validate this report", "cvss score".

buy-vs-build.routing:
Hall names the capability needed; the skill
researches 3-5 candidate vendors via /discover, scores each on Cost, Lock-in, Fit,
Risk, Time-to-value, and Team capacity. Recommends with rationale and a back-out
plan. Output is a single decision doc at `decisions/buy-vs-build/{slug}.md` that
can be linked from an ADR. Use when: "buy vs build", "should we use Stripe", "make
or buy", "vendor decision", "is X worth building".
buy-vs-build.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "buy vs build", "make or buy", "should we use".

c4-diagram.lead: [Chief Architect] Generates the four C4 model levels (System Context, Container, Component, Code) from the actual codebase, not whiteboard memory.
c4-diagram.routing:
Uses imports,
service boundaries, IaC, and OpenAPI specs as ground truth. Emits Mermaid and
structurizr-DSL so diagrams can be rendered in docs or pushed to a Structurizr
workspace. Use when: "C4 diagram", "architecture diagram", "draw the system",
"context container component", "structurizr", or "generate architecture docs".

canary.routing:
Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy".

careful.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Safety guardrails for destructive commands.
careful.routing:
Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,
force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations.
User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems,
or working in a shared environment. Use when asked to "be careful", "safety mode",
"prod mode", or "careful mode".

chaos.lead: [Cloud-Native] Design and run chaos engineering experiments per dependency-graph node.
chaos.routing:
Generates Litmus / Chaos Mesh experiments (pod kill, network delay,
CPU stress, dep failure), defines steady-state hypotheses from existing SLOs,
runs in staging, and measures actual SLO impact. Requires /sre-slo and a
dependency graph. Use when: "chaos engineering", "fault injection experiments",
"game day", "resilience testing", "litmus", "chaos mesh".

check-ci.lead: [Investigate & Security] One-shot CI status check for gstack.
check-ci.routing:
Queries GitHub Actions (or GitLab CI) for recent
workflow runs, reports failures, and suggests fixes. No daemon — run manually
when you want to know if CI is green.
Use when asked to "check ci", "ci status", "are builds passing", "check-ci",
or "what's failing in CI".

check-deps.lead: [Investigate & Security] One-shot dependency audit for gstack.
check-deps.routing:
Runs npm audit, pip audit, or equivalent for the
detected package manager. Reports vulnerabilities by severity with fix
recommendations. No daemon — run manually when you want a dependency health check.
Use when asked to "check deps", "audit dependencies", "check-deps",
"security audit deps", or "are my packages safe".

check-issues.lead: [Investigate & Security] One-shot issue triage for gstack.
check-issues.routing:
Queries GitHub Issues (or GitLab) for open issues,
categorizes by priority, and suggests which to tackle next. No daemon — run
manually when you want to see what needs attention.
Use when asked to "check issues", "triage issues", "check-issues",
"what's open", "issue backlog", or "what should I work on next".

clone-and-twist.lead: [Product Transposition] Build the transposed version of a studied mechanism.
clone-and-twist.routing:
Reads BLUEPRINT.md + TRANSPOSITION.md from studies/<slug>/, produces an
/autoplan-full-compatible build plan with core skeleton, domain adapters,
replacement modules, and pre-stubbed proprietary slots — then hands off to
/autobuild for execution. Writes mechanism-fidelity tests to
evals/fidelity/<slug>.jsonl consumed by /eval-harness (S13). Refuses to operate
on a study without a non-trivial domain shift + ≥1 proprietary slot.
Maintains BUILD-LOG.md linking commits back to blueprint sections.
Use when asked to "build the transposed version", "clone and twist this",
"kick off the build from this transposition", or "scaffold the transposed code".

codex.lead: [Code Review] OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes.
codex.routing:
Code review: independent diff review via
codex review with pass/fail gate. Challenge: adversarial mode that tries to break
your code. Consult: ask codex anything with session continuity for follow-ups.
The "200 IQ autistic developer" second opinion. Use when asked to "codex review",
"codex challenge", "ask codex", "second opinion", or "consult codex".
codex.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "code x", "code ex", "get another opinion".

cognitive-load-audit.lead: [Cognitive Computing / HMC] Quantifies cognitive load on a surface using Sweller's intrinsic / extraneous / germane split.
cognitive-load-audit.routing:
Counts decisions per screen, working-memory
chunks, novelty rate, and choice overload signals; identifies which load type is
dominant and proposes cuts targeted at that type. Live capture via /browse when
available, plan/spec analysis otherwise. Surfaces tradeoffs (simplicity vs.
capability, defaults vs. agency) rather than enforcing a "remove things" reflex.
Use when asked to "audit cognitive load", "this feels heavy", "too many options",
"simplify this screen", or "is this overwhelming".

collaboration-pattern.lead: [Cognitive Computing / HMC] Picks the right human-AI collaboration pattern (pair, supervised, delegated, autonomous) for a given task, with rationale across skill-level fit, error cost,...
collaboration-pattern.routing:
Generates
the prompt scaffolding and UI affordances for the chosen pattern. References
Engelbart's augmentation framework, Suchman's situated action, and recent HCAI
literature (Shneiderman, Amershi, Bansal). Presents the four patterns as a
continuum, not a hierarchy.
Use when asked to "pick a collaboration pattern", "should this be autonomous",
"how much should the AI do", "pair vs delegate", or "design the human-AI
hand-off".

compliance-audit.lead: [CISO Compliance + IR] Framework-aware compliance audit (SOC 2 Type II / ISO 27001 / HIPAA / PCI-DSS v4 / GDPR / CCPA).
compliance-audit.routing:
Walks each control with the framework citation,
maps existing artifacts (CI configs, IAM policies, runbooks, /cso findings) to
evidence, scores Pass / Partial / Gap per control, and emits a Plan-of-Action-and-
Milestones (POA&M) with owners and target dates. Designed for the founder-CISO
who has to answer an enterprise security questionnaire before the next renewal.
Use when: "compliance audit", "SOC 2 readiness", "HIPAA gap analysis",
"ISO 27001 control walk", "PCI-DSS assessment", "GDPR readiness", "POA&M".

context-restore.lead: [Intelligence & Memory] Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save.
context-restore.routing:
Loads the most recent
saved state (across all branches by default) so you can pick up where you
left off — even across Conductor workspace handoffs.
Use when asked to "resume", "restore context", "where was I", or
"pick up where I left off". Pair with /context-save.
Formerly /checkpoint resume — renamed because Claude Code treats /checkpoint
as a native rewind alias in current environments.

context-save.routing:
Captures git state, decisions made, and remaining work
so any future session can pick up without losing a beat.
Use when asked to "save progress", "save state", "context save", or
"save my work". Pair with /context-restore to resume later.
Formerly /checkpoint — renamed because Claude Code treats /checkpoint as a
native rewind alias in current environments, which was shadowing this skill.

cost-optimize.lead: [Chief Architect] FinOps analysis for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
cost-optimize.routing:
Pulls Cost Explorer /
Cloud Billing / Cost Management, ranks the top spend, finds rightsizing
candidates, reserved-instance vs Savings-Plan picks, spot eligibility, and
idle resources. Emits an action list with $/month savings per item, sorted by
ratio of savings to effort. Read-only by default; tag/stop suggestions are
proposals, not actions. Use when: "cost optimize", "finops review", "cloud bill
is up", "find idle resources", "should we buy reserved instances", or "cost
attribution".

counterfactual.lead: [Economic Simulation] Counterfactual analysis framework for economic simulators.
counterfactual.routing:
Runs
paired baseline / intervention simulations across a multi-seed ensemble, computes
confidence bands via bootstrap or quantile aggregation, performs sensitivity analysis
on key parameters, and produces a structured counterfactual report. Enforces
multi-seed discipline — single-run counterfactuals are rejected because they hide
Monte Carlo uncertainty. Pairs with `/econ-sim` (consumes the sim), `/event-stream`
(replays or mutates the event log to encode the intervention), and `/sim-calibrate`
(uses the same sim but for fitting rather than asking "what if").
Use when asked to "run a counterfactual", "what if the central bank had raised
rates 6 months earlier", "policy analysis on the sim", "intervention analysis",
"paired-run counterfactual", or "sensitivity analysis on an econ sim".

cross-repo-pr.lead: [Multi-Repo] Atomic PR trains across N repos that must merge in lockstep.
cross-repo-pr.routing:
Defines a merge
gate (all green + all approved), lands PRs in dependency order with retry on conflict,
and reverts the WHOLE train automatically if any post-merge production check fails.
Use when asked to "atomic multi-repo PR", "PR train", "lockstep merge", "ship across repos
together", or "merge these PRs as a unit".

cso.routing:
Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,
dependency supply chain, CI/CD pipeline security, LLM/AI security, skill supply chain
scanning, plus OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification.
Two modes: daily (zero-noise, 8/10 confidence gate) and comprehensive (monthly deep
scan, 2/10 bar). Trend tracking across audit runs.
Use when: "security audit", "threat model", "pentest review", "OWASP", "CSO review".
cso.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "see-so", "see so", "security review", "security check", "vulnerability scan", "run security".

customer-interview.lead: [CEO Cockpit] Customer interview workflow — two modes.
customer-interview.routing:
**Prep mode** generates a JTBD-style
question script for a target persona. **Synthesis mode** ingests transcripts (or audio
via `markitdown`), extracts pain points, segments by job-to-be-done, scores by
frequency × intensity, and proposes 3 concrete product moves. Cross-interview pattern
detection via `/intel` keeps learnings from rotting in Notion.
Use when: "customer interview prep", "synthesize the interview", "JTBD interview".
customer-interview.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "interview prep", "synthesize the call", "JTBD".

dashboard.routing:
Launches several review/QA skills
in parallel via Claude Code Agent Teams (or sequential fallback), renders
live progress as a terminal box-drawing table, then merges per-skill output
into a unified scorecard. Three built-in presets: review (cso + review +
codex), qa (qa + design-review), full (all reviews + qa + audit). Resource-
capped at 3 parallel agents; one crash never takes the others down.
Use when asked to "run dashboard", "parallel review", "review everything",
"dashboard preset", or "multi-agent review".
dashboard.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dashboard", "parallel review", "run them all".

db-design.lead: [API/DB] Design a new database schema or audit an existing one.
db-design.routing:
Knows naming
conventions, normalization vs denormalization, index choice, partitioning,
soft-delete patterns, append-only vs mutable history, JSON-column tradeoffs.
Outputs forward migration SQL plus rollback. For audits, scores the schema
against rules and produces a remediation plan. Supports Postgres, SQLite, and
MySQL. Use when: "design a schema", "audit our schema", "review this migration",
"should this be a JSON column", "normalization review".

db-migration.routing:
Given a migration intent
(add column, change type, add index, rename, backfill, drop), picks the safe
pattern — expand-migrate-contract, online DDL, pt-osc / gh-ost for MySQL,
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for Postgres — and emits phased migration scripts
with locks-and-load analysis plus rollback at every phase. Supports Postgres,
SQLite, and MySQL. Use when: "safely migrate this table", "add NOT NULL
column", "rename a column on a live table", "add index without blocking",
or "zero-downtime migration".

db-perf.lead: [API/DB] EXPLAIN-driven slow-query diagnosis and index advisor.
db-perf.routing:
Extracts candidate
queries from app code and DB logs, runs EXPLAIN ANALYZE against a read-only
dev/staging DB, spots seq scans, missing indexes, N+1 patterns, suboptimal joins,
and bad statistics. Suggests indexes with cost estimates and predicted impact.
Supports Postgres, SQLite, and MySQL. Use when: "the API is slow", "diagnose
this query", "missing index", "p99 spike", "explain plan", or "advise indexes".

define-workflows.lead: [UX Audit] Derive the list of workflows each persona *should* be able to accomplish, based on their goal, permissions, and entry point.
define-workflows.routing:
Produces one expected-
workflows file per persona at .gstack/feature-build/expected-workflows-
{persona}-{datetime}.md. This is the input to /gap-analysis and
/auto-feature-build — a lightweight standalone entry point for Step A of
feature-build gap analysis, extracted so you can enumerate workflows
without running the full build pipeline.
Use when asked to "define workflows", "list expected workflows", "what
should each persona be able to do", or "enumerate workflows per persona".

dep-graph.lead: [Chief Architect] Builds a cross-service dependency graph from imports, route calls, and IaC topology.
dep-graph.routing:
Detects cycles, identifies bottleneck nodes, and
computes blast radius for any node ("what does this change affect"). Stores
the graph in ~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/graph.json for /c4-diagram,
/migration-plan, and /service-catalog to consume. Emits Markdown + SVG +
JSON. Use when: "dependency graph", "what does this affect", "blast radius",
"find cycles", "what imports X", or "build a dep graph".

design-consultation.lead: [Design] Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and generates...
design-consultation.routing:
Creates DESIGN.md as your project's design source
of truth. For existing sites, use /plan-design-review to infer the system instead.
Use when asked to "design system", "brand guidelines", or "create DESIGN.md".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI with no existing
design system or DESIGN.md.

design-html.lead: [Design] Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.
design-html.routing:
Works with approved mockups from /design-shotgun, CEO plans from /plan-ceo-review,
design review context from /plan-design-review, or from scratch with a user
description. Text actually reflows, heights are computed, layouts are dynamic.
30KB overhead, zero deps. Smart API routing: picks the right Pretext patterns
for each design type. Use when: "finalize this design", "turn this into HTML",
"build me a page", "implement this design", or after any planning skill.
Proactively suggest when user has approved a design or has a plan ready.
design-html.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "build the design", "code the mockup", "make it real".

design-review.lead: [Design] Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems, AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them.
design-review.routing:
Iteratively fixes issues
in source code, committing each fix atomically and re-verifying with before/after
screenshots. For plan-mode design review (before implementation), use /plan-design-review.
Use when asked to "audit the design", "visual QA", "check if it looks good", or "design polish".
Proactively suggest when the user mentions visual inconsistencies or
wants to polish the look of a live site.

design-shotgun.lead: [Design] Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board, collect structured feedback, and iterate.
design-shotgun.routing:
Standalone design exploration you can
run anytime. Use when: "explore designs", "show me options", "design variants",
"visual brainstorm", or "I don't like how this looks".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a UI feature but hasn't seen
what it could look like.

detect-engineering.routing:
Given a threat indicator
(ATT&CK technique, malware family, IoC bundle, behavioral pattern), generates
one rule per detection dialect — Sigma (universal source), KQL (Microsoft
Defender / Sentinel), SPL (Splunk), EQL (Elastic), YARA (file artifacts),
Suricata (network), Zeek (network). Every rule ships with FP-reduction
modifiers and concrete test cases. Refuses to run without an
`~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml` ownership declaration.
Use when: "write a detection", "Sigma rule for X", "convert this to KQL",
"detection engineering", "FP-tune this rule".

devex-review.routing:
Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the
developer experience: navigates docs, tries the getting started flow, times
TTHW, screenshots error messages, evaluates CLI help text. Produces a DX
scorecard with evidence. Compares against /plan-devex-review scores if they
exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature.
devex-review.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx audit", "test the developer experience", "try the onboarding", "developer experience test".

discover.lead: [CTO Discovery] CTO-mode technology and product intelligence for gstack.
discover.routing:
Scans competitors, scouts
emerging tech, finds market gaps, and delivers opinionated recommendations.
Not a report generator — a visionary who synthesizes and recommends with
conviction. Output is a research brief consumable by downstream skills.
Use when asked to "research competitors", "what's out there", "tech scout",
"discover alternatives", "market analysis", "build vs buy", or "landscape scan".
Proactively suggest when the user is exploring a new product area,
evaluating technology choices, or asking "what should we use for X".

discover-personas.lead: [UX Audit] Discover and register personas for the project, either from the user's description or by auto-discovering them from the live app.
discover-personas.routing:
Writes the shared
persona registry at .gstack/ux-audit/personas.md, which is consumed by
/ux-audit, /auto-ux-audit, /auto-ux-audit-full, /feature-build,
/auto-feature-build, and /auto-feature-build-full. Lightweight standalone
entry point — same logic as /ux-audit Phase 1, extracted so you can bootstrap
the registry without running a full audit.
Use when asked to "discover personas", "who uses this app", "find the user
roles", "bootstrap personas", or "set up the persona registry".

document-generate.lead: [Ship & Release] Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project.
document-generate.routing:
Uses the Diataxis framework (tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation) to produce
complete, structured documentation. Can be invoked standalone or called by
/document-release when it finds coverage gaps. Use when asked to "write docs",
"generate documentation", "document this feature", "create a tutorial", or
"explain this module".

document-release.routing:
Reads all project docs, cross-references the
diff, builds a Diataxis coverage map (reference/how-to/tutorial/explanation),
updates README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md to match what shipped,
detects architecture diagram drift, polishes CHANGELOG voice with a sell-test
rubric, cleans up TODOS, and optionally bumps VERSION. Surfaces documentation
debt in the PR body. Use when asked to "update the docs", "sync documentation",
or "post-ship docs". Proactively suggest after a PR is merged or code is shipped.

econ-sim.lead: [Economic Simulation] Designs a runnable economic simulator from a research question.
econ-sim.routing:
Picks paradigm — agent-based (Mesa / Agents.jl) for heterogeneous agents and emergent
behavior, system dynamics (PySD) for aggregate stock-flow, DSGE (Dynare) for
general-equilibrium policy analysis, or hybrid for cross-scale. Specifies agent classes
(households / firms / banks / govt / external), interaction protocol, time discretization,
market clearing, and a reproducibility envelope (seed, config, git SHA pinned per run).
Pairs with `/event-stream` for live event ingestion, `/counterfactual` for paired-run
policy analysis, and `/sim-calibrate` for historical fit.
Use when asked to "design an economic simulation", "build an ABM", "pick a sim
paradigm", "agent-based macro model", "DSGE model", "system dynamics model", or
"stand up a runnable economic simulator".

eval-harness.lead: [AI Engineering / Research] Generates a per-feature eval harness (dataset, metrics, baselines, CI hook) for any LLM/AI feature.
eval-harness.routing:
Picks pytest/deepeval/ragas/promptfoo per fit. Supports
--domain {nlp, cv, speech-tts, speech-asr, multimodal, sign}. Each domain pins
the right datasets, metrics, and judge model. Writes typed records to the
shared S9 eval store so /rag-design, /agent-design, /finetune, and
/prompt-engineering can compare across runs. Use when: "set up evals", "add
evals for this feature", "regression test for an AI feature", "I need a
benchmark for this model".
eval-harness.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "eval harness", "evaluation harness", "set up evals".

event-design.lead: [API/DB] Design event/message schemas with explicit versioning and compatibility policy for Kafka, NATS, EventBridge, SQS, RabbitMQ, or Pub/Sub.
event-design.routing:
Generates Avro,
JSON Schema, or Protobuf. Audits existing topics for compat violations and
registry hygiene. Knows forward / backward / full compatibility rules and the
trade-offs per format. Use when: "design event schemas", "kafka schemas",
"avro vs protobuf vs json schema", "schema registry", or "are these events
backwards compatible".

event-stream.lead: [Economic Simulation] Designs a real-world event ingestion pipeline for an economic simulator with observability-time modeling — agents react to events when the information would have been...
event-stream.routing:
Sources span
news, social signal, macro indicators (FRED), policy announcements, and market data
(Alpha Vantage). Outputs a normalized event schema, an observability map per agent
class, an ingestion plan with replay vs live-fire modes, and a reproducibility envelope
per run. Pairs with `/econ-sim`, `/counterfactual`, and `/sim-calibrate`.
Use when asked to "design an event stream", "ingest real-world events into a sim",
"model information arrival", "news / macro / market event pipeline", "observability-time
event modeling", or "replay vs live event ingestion".

explanation-design.lead: [Cognitive Computing / HMC] Designs explanation surfaces for AI features with the goal of trust calibration, not tutorial.
explanation-design.routing:
Picks the right format (inline / on-demand /
progressive) per user task, and scopes the five trust dimensions: confidence,
alternatives, provenance, controllability, recourse. References Doshi-Velez & Kim,
Miller, Lipton, Wachter on counterfactual recourse. Surfaces tradeoffs between
faithfulness, intelligibility, and attention budget rather than enforcing a fixed
explanation style.
Use when asked to "design the explanation", "explain this AI feature", "trust
surfaces", "why did the model say that", "should we add an explanation", or
"calibrate trust".

exploit-dev.lead: [Red Team / RE] Scaffolds an authorized exploit-development project (CTF or engagement) for binary pwn, web, mobile, or IoT firmware targets.
exploit-dev.routing:
Generates pwntools / radare2 / angr
starter scripts, Makefile, journal template, and a structured README. Two modes:
--mode ctf (relaxed, for capture-the-flag and personal practice) and --mode engagement
(strict, requires engagements/{slug}/scope.yaml with the target in scope and authorized
techniques covering exploitation). Every action hash-chained into audit.log.
Use when: "exploit dev", "pwn this binary", "ctf scaffold", "rop chain template",
"pwntools setup", "angr harness", "exploit project".
exploit-dev.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "exploit dev", "pwn scaffold", "start exploit".

expression-synth.routing:
Builds a control
surface combining continuous valence-arousal, conversational-beat markers
(back-channels, turn-taking cues), intent-driven micro-expressions
(curiosity, doubt, recognition), and FACS-based action unit composition.
Picks the right backbone (EMOCA / DECA / FACSHuman / FLAME-based regressors)
per face rig and writes a build-ready plan. Outputs CONTROL-SURFACE +
EMOTION-MODEL + BEAT-CUES + FACS-MAP + EVAL docs.
Use when: "facial expression synthesis", "emotion model for avatar", "FACS map",
"conversational beats", "micro-expressions", or "expression control surface".
expression-synth.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "expression synthesis", "facial expression", "emotion model", "facs map", "micro expressions".

feature-build.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Single-feature manual builder for gstack.
feature-build.routing:
The user specifies exactly ONE feature they want
built; feature-build runs it through the full pipeline: (optionally) frame it
with office-hours, plan it with autoplan-full (deep framing) or autoplan (fast),
build it with autobuild, verify with verify-loop, then scoped workflow
verification + autoaudit. One feature, maximum rigor. No auto-discovery.
Use when asked to "build this feature", "feature build", "take this feature
end-to-end", or "one feature through the pipeline". For auto-discovery of
missing workflows, use /auto-feature-build. For all personas, use
/auto-feature-build-full.

finetune.lead: [AI Engineering / Research] Generates a fine-tuning project — data prep, format conversion (sharegpt/alpaca/dpo), recipe config (LoRA / QLoRA / full / DPO / ORPO), eval baseline before+after,...
finetune.routing:
Supports --domain {nlp, cv,
speech-tts, speech-asr, multimodal, sign}. Framework-agnostic: names Axolotl,
TRL, Unsloth, HuggingFace, Diffusers as CHOICES per task type.
Outputs finetune/{model-slug}/{prepare.py,config.yaml,evals/,RECIPE.md}.
Use when: "fine-tune this model", "LoRA for X", "DPO recipe", "train an
adapter".
finetune.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "fine tune", "finetune", "train an adapter".

forensics.lead: [Blue Team / Defensive] DFIR (digital forensics + incident response).
forensics.routing:
Walks a case end-to-end: disk image acquisition (dd / FTK Imager /
Velociraptor agent), memory capture (Volatility 3), filesystem timeline
(Plaso / log2timeline), browser-artifact extraction, process-tree
reconstruction, IoC extraction. Produces a hash-chained chain-of-custody
log, structured findings, and an IoC handoff to /threat-intel and
/detect-engineering. STRICT READ-ONLY on evidence; mutations only in
working-copy. Refuses to run without
`~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml` + per-case explicit
authorization.
Use when: "DFIR", "forensics case", "analyze this disk image", "memory
analysis", "build a timeline of compromise", "chain of custody".

freeze.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session.
freeze.routing:
Blocks Edit and
Write outside the allowed path. Use when debugging to prevent accidentally
"fixing" unrelated code, or when you want to scope changes to one module.
Use when asked to "freeze", "restrict edits", "only edit this folder",
or "lock down edits".

game-theory-analysis.lead: [Operations Research / Behavioral] Model a strategic situation as a formal game (normal form for one-shot, extensive form for sequential).
game-theory-analysis.routing:
Identifies players, action
spaces, payoffs, and the right equilibrium concept (Nash, subgame-perfect, dominant-
strategy, correlated, evolutionary stable). Generates the payoff matrix, computes
equilibria with nashpy / gambit, surfaces dominated strategies, credible threats,
and commitment opportunities. Use for pricing wars, feature races, negotiation prep,
competitive responses, and public-commitment design.
Use when: "game theory", "nash equilibrium", "payoff matrix", "competitive response",
"negotiation prep", "credible threat", "dominant strategy".

gap-analysis.lead: [UX Audit] Compare expected workflows (from /define-workflows) against what the app actually supports.
gap-analysis.routing:
Produces one gap report per persona at
.gstack/feature-build/gap-report-{persona}-{datetime}.md with a
completeness rubric (route, form, handler, persist, feedback — 5 signals
each 0/1) and a verdict (PRESENT_COMPLETE, PRESENT_BROKEN, PRESENT_PARTIAL,
HIDDEN, ABSENT). Lightweight standalone entry point for Steps B+C+D of
feature-build gap analysis, extracted so you can see what's missing
without running the full build pipeline.
Use when asked to "gap analysis", "what's missing", "find gaps in the
app", "completeness check", or "what does each persona still need".

gesture-synth.routing:
Picks the right
co-speech-gesture model (GENEA SOTA / Audio2Gestures / GestureDiffusion /
ZeroEGGS / DiffuseStyleGesture) per use case (conversational / presenter /
character), builds a cultural-gesture vocabulary (beats / deictics / iconic /
metaphoric), and writes a build-ready plan with retargeting strategy to the
avatar body rig. Outputs MODEL + VOCAB + STYLE + EVAL docs.
Use when: "co-speech gestures", "gesture synthesis", "speech-to-motion",
"presenter gestures", or "audio-to-body animation".
gesture-synth.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "co speech gestures", "gesture synthesis", "speech to gesture", "presenter gestures".

gstack.lead: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding.
gstack.routing:
Navigate pages, interact with
elements, verify state, diff before/after, take annotated screenshots, test responsive
layouts, forms, uploads, dialogs, and capture bug evidence. Use when asked to open or
test a site, verify a deployment, dogfood a user flow, or file a bug with screenshots.

gstack-upgrade.lead: [Tools] Upgrade gstack to the latest version.
gstack-upgrade.routing:
Detects global vs vendored install,
runs the upgrade, and shows what's new. Use when asked to "upgrade gstack",
"update gstack", or "get latest version".
gstack-upgrade.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "upgrade the tools", "update the tools", "gee stack upgrade", "g stack upgrade".

guard.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
guard.routing:
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety".

health.routing:
Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score".

hiring-loop.lead: [CTO Strategy] Full hiring kit generator from a role description.
hiring-loop.routing:
Produces JD with
leveling, async screen prompt, 4-stage interview kit (technical, system design,
behavioral, founder-fit), and per-stage scorecards. Mode flag for IC vs lead.
All four artifacts stay aligned to the same competency rubric so signal doesn't
drift between stages. Output: `hiring/{role-slug}/{JD,SCREEN,INTERVIEW,SCORECARD}.md`.
Use when: "write a JD", "hiring kit", "interview kit for senior engineer", "screen
prompt for backend role", "scorecard for a tech lead role".
hiring-loop.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "hiring kit", "write a jd", "interview loop".

honeypot.routing:
Three tiers: (1) canary
tokens (AWS keys, IAM users, fake env files, watermarked docs) via
canarytokens.org or self-hosted Thinkst; (2) honeypages (fake admin
panels, planted API keys in code) seeded on attacker discovery paths;
(3) full honeypots via T-Pot or Thinkst Canary. Monitors triggers, alerts
on hit, suggests follow-up (rotate adjacent real creds, block source IP,
escalate to /forensics). Refuses to run without
`~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml` AND explicit cloud-
account + IAM scope declaration.
Use when: "deploy canary tokens", "set up honeypot", "deception", "fake
credentials for attackers".

iac-review.lead: [Chief Architect] Pre-apply review of Terraform / Pulumi / AWS CDK diffs.
iac-review.routing:
Skips
the 200 lines of resource-rename noise to surface the things that actually
matter: destructive changes (deletes, replaces), IAM widenings, public exposure,
instance-class upgrades over 2x cost, missing tags, drift from the policy
baseline. Default is dry-run; --apply requires explicit confirmation. Use when:
"review this terraform plan", "iac review", "is this plan safe to apply",
"review the cdk diff", or "review the pulumi preview".

incentive-design.lead: [Operations Research / Behavioral] Design an incentive system (pricing tier, referral program, growth loop, OKR / commission plan, in-app reward) using mechanism design plus behavioral economics —...
incentive-design.routing:
Produces (a) the incentive structure, (b) the predicted
behavior, and (c) a MANDATORY failure-mode list (gaming, perverse incentives, second-
order effects, Goodhart). Cross-domain orchestrator for pricing + marketing-psychology.
Use when: "design the incentive", "design the OKR", "design the referral program",
"design the commission plan", "design pricing tiers", "mechanism design".

incident-response.lead: [CISO Compliance + IR] Generates an incident response runbook tuned to Hall's stack (web / mobile / API), and runs in live war-room mode during an actual incident.
incident-response.routing:
Walks the operator through declaration, triage, mitigation, comms,
and resolution. Includes severity rubric (SEV-1 to SEV-4), comms templates
(customer, internal, status page), on-call rotation suggestions, and a clean
hand-off to /postmortem. Maps to SOC 2 CC7.3-7.5, ISO 27001 A.5.24-A.5.27,
HIPAA §164.308(a)(6), PCI-DSS Req 12.10, GDPR Art. 33-34 (72-hour breach clock).
Use when: "incident response", "we have an outage", "we're breached",
"incident runbook", "war room", "SEV-1", "page the on-call".

indoor-nav.lead: [Accessibility AI] Indoor positioning + accessibility-aware routing design.
indoor-nav.routing:
Picks the sensor stack (Wi-Fi RTT, BLE beacons, IMU dead-reckoning, magnetic
anomaly, visual SLAM), the localization algorithm (particle filter, EKF/UKF,
factor graph), the indoor map data model (with elevator/stairs/ramp/door-width
annotations), the accessibility routing constraints (elevator preference,
wheelchair width, slope limits, no stairs), and the turn-by-turn UX for
vision-impaired and mobility-impaired users. Outputs a build-ready architecture
per venue type (mall, hospital, campus, transit hub).
Use when asked to "design indoor nav", "indoor positioning", "wayfinding
system", "accessible routing", "where am I indoors", or "build indoor
navigation".

intel.lead: [Intelligence & Memory] Project intelligence briefing — reads cross-session memory from intelligence.jsonl, compacts stale entries, and presents a briefing of
intel.routing:
recurring patterns, recent findings,
and known hotspots. Run at session start for full context, or anytime to check what
gstack has learned about this project.
Use when asked to "show intel", "what do you know", "project briefing",
"intelligence report", or "project intel".
Proactively suggest at session start if intelligence data exists.

interactive-mockup.lead: [UX Pipeline] Wire the static mockup into a clickable interactive prototype.
interactive-mockup.routing:
Reads docs/WORKFLOWS.md and mockup/screens/. Adds data-action attributes to relevant
buttons/links/forms; writes mockup/interactions.js with handlers for navigation,
fake form success, modals, dropdowns, multi-step flow state machines, and mock-data
binding from mockup/data/*.json. Pure vanilla JS, zero npm deps. Layer 6 of
MOCKUP-ARCHITECTURE.md. Stakeholders get a clickable Figma-alike preview, not a slideshow.
Use when asked to "make the mockup interactive", "wire the clicks", "interactive mockup",
"add behavior", or "the mockup should respond".
Proactively suggest after /mockup completes, before stakeholder review.

investigate.lead: [Investigate & Security] Systematic debugging with root cause investigation.
investigate.routing:
Four phases: investigate,
analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause.
Use when asked to "debug this", "fix this bug", "why is this broken",
"investigate this error", or "root cause analysis".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT debug directly) when the user reports
errors, 500 errors, stack traces, unexpected behavior, "it was working
yesterday", or is troubleshooting why something stopped working.

investigate-workflow.lead: [Investigate & Security] Post-implementation workflow completeness investigator for gstack.
investigate-workflow.routing:
Traces every code path
in a feature area end-to-end — routes, handlers, APIs, state transitions, UI
triggers — and reports what's broken, incomplete, or disconnected. Unlike
/investigate (debugs a known bug) or /plan-ux-review (reviews a plan), this
skill discovers problems you don't know about yet by walking every workflow path.
Use when asked to "check if this feature is complete", "verify the workflow",
"trace the flow", "what's missing", or "is this fully wired up".
Proactively suggest when Claude has just finished building a multi-step feature,
or when the user suspects something was partially implemented.

investor-update.routing:
Reads git log, gh PRs, Stripe/Plausible/PostHog
metrics via configured connectors, prior `/retro` outputs, and the last update. Drafts
the next update in Hall's voice with Highlights / Lowlights / Metrics / Asks. Stops
at AskUserQuestion gates for tone calibration and the ask list. Runs in ~10 minutes
what used to take a full day to reconstruct.
Use when: "investor update", "monthly update for investors", "draft the LP update".
investor-update.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "investor update", "draft the update", "monthly update".

ios-clean.lead: Remove the DebugBridge SPM package and all #if DEBUG wiring from an iOS app.
ios-clean.routing:
Cleans up StateServer, DebugOverlay, accessor codegen output, and
app-side hooks installed by /ios-qa. This is a convenience wrapper —
the structural Release-build guard (Package.swift conditional + CI
swift build -c release check) is the safety-critical path.
Use when asked to "clean the iOS debug bridge", "remove DebugBridge",
or "strip the gstack iOS instrumentation".
ios-clean.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "clean the iOS debug bridge", "remove DebugBridge", "strip the gstack iOS instrumentation".

ios-design-review.lead: Visual design audit for iOS apps on real hardware.
ios-design-review.routing:
Connects to a real
iPhone via the same StateServer as /ios-qa, screenshots every screen,
evaluates against Apple HIG, DESIGN.md, and design best practices. Scores
each dimension 0-10 with "what would make it a 10" framing — mirrors
/plan-design-review for browser. For plan-stage design review (before
implementation), use /plan-design-review. For live web visual audits, use
/design-review.
Use when asked to "review the iOS design", "audit the iPhone app's
visuals", or "design QA the iOS app".
