redteam-c2.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "redteam c2", "c2 setup", "engagement infrastructure".

retro.routing:
Analyzes commit history, work patterns,
and code quality metrics with persistent history and trend tracking.
Team-aware: breaks down per-person contributions with praise and growth areas.
Use when asked to "weekly retro", "what did we ship", or "engineering retrospective".
Proactively suggest at the end of a work week or sprint.

reverse-engineer.routing:
Drives Ghidra headless (or Cutter) over Mach-O /
ELF / PE binaries — extracts strings, functions, xrefs, suspicious calls, anti-debug,
packer signatures — annotated report with next-step hypotheses. Mobile: jadx (Android),
class-dump (iOS). Refuses without engagements/{slug}/scope.yaml in scope (engagement) or
owned-artifact declaration (research on Hall's own binaries / CTF challenges).
Use when: "reverse engineer", "analyze this binary", "ghidra headless", "decompile",
"unpack", "android apk decompile", "mach-o analysis". 
Distinct from /blueprint (Family J — constructive RE on public material); this is
*adversarial* RE on scope-gated targets.
reverse-engineer.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "reverse engineer", "analyze binary", "decompile this".

review.routing:
Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust
boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues. Use when
asked to "review this PR", "code review", "pre-landing review", or "check my diff".
Proactively suggest when the user is about to merge or land code changes.

risk-engine.lead: [FinTech / Algorithmic Trading] Risk engine designer for algorithmic strategies.
risk-engine.routing:
Lays
down Kelly / fractional-Kelly / risk-parity / vol-targeted position sizing,
per-asset and per-factor caps, daily / weekly / monthly drawdown triggers, a kill
switch with manual interlock, and a re-backtest of the strategy WITH the risk
overlay applied. Enforces the "no naked strategy" rule: a backtest may exist
without a risk engine; a paper trade or live trade may not.
Use when: "design risk engine", "position sizing", "kill switch",
"drawdown limits", "Kelly", "risk overlay".
risk-engine.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "design risk engine", "position sizing", "kill switch".

runway.lead: [CEO Cockpit] Burn + runway + hire-cost scenario modeler.
runway.routing:
Reads bank balance + monthly burn +
revenue from a configured ledger source (Stripe + Mercury/Brex CSV). Models scenarios:
"if I hire X at $Y", "if revenue grows N% MoM", "if we cut Z". Deterministic math in
`lib/runway-model.ts` — not an LLM guess. Outputs a one-page report with scenario
tables and a runway chart.
Use when: "what's our runway", "model the hire", "burn analysis", "runway scenarios".
runway.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "what's our runway", "model the hire", "burn analysis".

scrape.lead: [Browser & Web] Pull data from a web page.
scrape.routing:
First call on a new intent prototypes the flow
via $B primitives and returns JSON. Subsequent calls on a matching intent
route to a codified browser-skill and return in ~200ms. Read-only — for
mutating flows (form fills, clicks, submissions), use /automate.
Use when asked to "scrape", "get data from", "pull", "extract from", or
"what's on" a page.

security-training.lead: [CISO Compliance + IR] Project-specific security training generator.
security-training.routing:
Replaces
generic SaaS training with materials that name Hall's actual vendors, cite
real diffs (good and bad) from the repo, and walk through the team's own
threat model. Outputs markdown training decks + a quiz that doubles as a
training-completion record (SOC 2 CC1.4, ISO 27001 A.6.3, HIPAA §164.308(a)(5),
PCI-DSS Req 12.6, GDPR Art. 39(1)(b) awareness).
Use when: "security training", "phishing simulation", "secure coding training",
"awareness training", "training records for SOC 2", "annual training".

service-catalog.lead: [Chief Architect] One-time scaffolding of a Backstage-style service catalog in markdown.
service-catalog.routing:
Per-service entry with owner, SLOs, runbook link, on-call rotation,
dependencies, data classification, and lifecycle state. Auto-detects services
from the codebase + IaC + container manifests, proposes entries for missing
services, and detects services that have been deleted. Keeps an INDEX.md with
ownership and search. Use when: "service catalog", "who owns this", "list our
services", "service ownership", or "backstage in markdown".

service-mesh.lead: [Cloud-Native] Decide whether a service mesh actually helps, and if so, generate config for Istio, Linkerd, or Consul Connect.
service-mesh.routing:
Covers mTLS, traffic policies,
retries, timeouts, circuit breakers, canary routing, and fault injection — with
a hard audit step that often concludes "you don't need a mesh." Use when:
"do we need a service mesh", "istio config", "linkerd setup", "mTLS between
services", "canary routing", "fault injection".

setup-browser-cookies.lead: [Browser & Web] Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session.
setup-browser-cookies.routing:
Opens an interactive picker UI where you select which cookie domains to import.
Use before QA testing authenticated pages. Use when asked to "import cookies",
"login to the site", or "authenticate the browser".

setup-deploy.routing:
Detects your deploy
platform (Fly.io, Render, Vercel, Netlify, Heroku, GitHub Actions, custom),
production URL, health check endpoints, and deploy status commands. Writes
the configuration to CLAUDE.md so all future deploys are automatic.
Use when: "setup deploy", "configure deployment", "set up land-and-deploy",
"how do I deploy with gstack", "add deploy config".

setup-gbrain.lead: [Intelligence & Memory] Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a local PGLite or Supabase brain, register MCP, capture per-remote trust policy.
setup-gbrain.routing:
One command from zero to "gbrain is running, and this agent
can call it." Use when: "setup gbrain", "connect gbrain", "start
gbrain", "install gbrain", "configure gbrain for this machine".

shared-lib-bump.lead: [Multi-Repo] Cascading version-bump PRs across every detected consumer of a shared lib.
shared-lib-bump.routing:
Detects consumers via /dep-graph or by scanning lockfiles, opens a bump PR per consumer,
runs each consumer's test suite, and files ONE tracking issue with the merge plan
(not N — the issue is the rollup).
Use when asked to "bump shared lib", "cascade version bump", "roll out new version",
"upgrade @org/lib everywhere", or "consumer rollout".

ship.lead: [Ship & Release] Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR.
ship.routing:
Use when asked to "ship", "deploy",
"push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push", or "get it deployed".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT push/PR directly) when the user says code
is ready, asks about deploying, wants to push code up, or asks to create a PR.

siem-tune.lead: [Blue Team / Defensive] SIEM rule fidelity tuning.
siem-tune.routing:
Audits the current SIEM
rule set against actual alert volume + analyst dispositions (TP / FP /
benign). Scores each rule on precision × business impact × adversary
plausibility. Proposes retirement, tuning (suppression conditions,
threshold changes), and new-rule gaps. Default mode is read-only audit;
`--apply` requires explicit AskUserQuestion approval per rule change.
Refuses to run without `~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml`.
Use when: "tune the SIEM", "kill noisy alerts", "FP reduction", "rule
fidelity scoring", "retire stale rules".

sign-linguistics.routing:
Builds the
structured resources that every downstream sign-language tool depends on:
handshape inventory (with HamNoSys / SignWriting / SLPA encoding), location
grid, movement primitives, non-manual marker conventions, prosody features
(intensity, repetition, hold, speed), and a primary-source bibliography.
One catalog per language — ASL, BSL, TSL, JSL, Auslan, LSF, DGS, Libras, KSL,
etc. Output is structured YAML consumed by /sign-text, /multi-sign, and
/avatar-sign.
Use when asked to "sign linguistics", "handshape inventory", "build sign
catalog", "sign phonology", or "HamNoSys / SignWriting encoding".

sign-text.lead: [Accessibility AI] Bidirectional text↔sign translation pipeline design.
sign-text.routing:
NOT
spoken-language MT. Signs decompose into handshape, location, movement,
orientation, and non-manual features (eye gaze, brows, mouthing, head tilt,
body lean). text→sign produces a parameter sequence renderable by an avatar
(Family P) or video synth; sign→text ingests video, extracts pose
(MediaPipe Holistic / Sapiens), segments signs, recognizes gloss, generates
text. Per target sign language: ASL, BSL, TSL, JSL, Auslan, LSF, DGS, etc.
Use when asked to "translate sign language", "text to sign", "sign to
text", "sign-language pipeline", or "build sign translation".

sim-calibrate.lead: [Economic Simulation] Calibration workflow for economic simulators.
sim-calibrate.routing:
Picks the calibration
target (moments, time series, or distributions), the distance metric (mean-squared
deviation, KL divergence, Wasserstein, or indirect inference), the parameter-search
algorithm (Bayesian via PyMC, grid, or surrogate-model-guided via scikit-learn), and
enforces train/test split discipline so the calibration target never overlaps the
held-out validation window. Pairs with `/econ-sim` (calibrates its parameters),
`/event-stream` (uses event streams as exogenous input with strict split boundary),
and `/counterfactual` (consumes the calibrated posterior).
Use when asked to "calibrate the sim", "fit the model to history", "Bayesian
calibration", "indirect inference", "moment matching", "match the simulator to data",
or "posterior over sim parameters".

simulate.lead: [Operations Research / Behavioral] Build a discrete-event or Monte-Carlo simulation of a system from a description.
simulate.routing:
Auto-generates a SimPy (or simmer) model, runs
scenarios, produces distribution outputs (p50/p95/p99), generates a tornado plot
for sensitivity, and persists a reproducible report. Use for capacity / pricing /
staffing / latency / queue / failure-rate questions where back-of-envelope math
hides the tail risk.
Use when: "simulate this", "monte carlo", "queueing analysis", "what does the
p99 look like", "tornado plot", "SimPy / simmer / discrete-event sim".

skillify.lead: [Intelligence & Memory] Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent browser-skill on disk.
skillify.routing:
Future /scrape calls with the same intent run
the codified script in ~200ms instead of re-driving the page. Walks
back through the conversation, synthesizes script.ts + script.test.ts
+ fixture, runs the test in a temp dir, and asks before committing.
Use when asked to "skillify", "codify", "save this scrape", or
"make this permanent".

soar-playbook.routing:
Outputs codified
incident-response playbooks for XSOAR / Tines / TheHive / Shuffle / n8n.
Covers phishing, credential-stuffed account, ransomware indicator,
public-facing-app exploit, insider data exfil. Each playbook: triage
steps, evidence collection, containment options (human-approval gated),
comms templates, IR handoff. Codified as YAML/JSON the target platform
imports, with a human-readable RUNBOOK.md companion. Refuses to run
without `~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml`.
Use when: "SOAR playbook", "automate IR for X", "XSOAR / Tines / TheHive
workflow", "incident response automation".

spec.lead: Turn vague intent into a precise, executable spec in five phases.
spec.routing:
Files the issue,
optionally spawns a Claude Code agent in a fresh worktree, and lets /ship close
the source issue on merge. Use when asked to "spec this out", "file an issue",
"write up a ticket", "make this a GitHub issue", or "turn this into a backlog item".

sre-slo.lead: [Cloud-Native] Define SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets per service — as engineering contracts, not marketing copy.
sre-slo.routing:
Picks the right SLI shape (request-based,
window-based, count-based) per workload, generates Prometheus recording +
alerting rules with multi-window burn-rate alerts, and writes a quarterly
review template. Use when: "define SLOs", "error budget", "set up SLIs",
"burn-rate alerts", "quarterly SLO review".

strategy-design.lead: [FinTech / Algorithmic Trading] Strategy design framework for algorithmic trading.
strategy-design.routing:
Forces an explicit edge hypothesis (microstructure / behavioral / informational / latency),
strategy class taxonomy (mean-reversion / momentum / stat-arb / market-making / ML),
signal generation, position-sizing rule, entry/exit logic, and risk-side constraints
BEFORE a single line of backtest code. Outputs a fully-specified strategy doc that
is testable, not an anecdote-driven hyperparameter fantasy.
Use when: "design a strategy", "new trading strategy", "edge hypothesis",
"strategy spec", "algo design".
strategy-design.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "design a strategy", "new trading strategy", "edge hypothesis".

strategy-eval.lead: [FinTech / Algorithmic Trading] Multi-metric strategy evaluation scorecard.
strategy-eval.routing:
Sharpe +
Sortino + Calmar + max drawdown + tail metrics (CVaR / expected shortfall),
regime-conditional performance (bull / bear / sideways / high-vol / low-vol),
capacity estimate (at what AUM does the edge break?), drawdown recovery analysis,
and live-vs-backtest divergence detection. Consumes trade history from /backtest,
/paper-trade, or live, and emits a scorecard a portfolio manager can act on.
Use when: "evaluate this strategy", "strategy scorecard", "Sharpe Sortino Calmar",
"tail risk", "regime breakdown", "capacity estimate".
strategy-eval.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "evaluate strategy", "strategy scorecard", "tail risk".

sync-gbrain.lead: [Intelligence & Memory] Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search guidance in CLAUDE.md. Wraps the gstack-gbrain-sync
sync-gbrain.routing:
orchestrator with
state probing, native code-surface registration, capability checks,
and a verdict block. Re-runnable, idempotent. Use when: "sync gbrain",
"refresh gbrain", "re-index this repo", "gbrain search isn't finding
things".

team-velocity.lead: [CTO Strategy] Throughput, cycle-time, review-latency, and bug-rate trend report for the last 12 weeks.
team-velocity.routing:
Pulls commit/PR/issue data via `gh` (per contributor or solo),
computes the stats, surfaces inflection points, and asks "what changed in week N?"
with cross-referenced context from `intel.jsonl`. Output: `metrics/velocity/{YYYY-WW}.md`.
Use when: "are we slowing down", "team velocity", "throughput report", "DORA metrics",
"what changed in week N".
team-velocity.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "team velocity", "are we slowing down", "throughput report".

tech-debt-register.routing:
Scans the codebase for debt markers
(TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX), correlates with `git blame` age and `/retro` recurring-
breakage signals from `intel.jsonl`, scores each item by **impact × frequency /
effort**, and outputs a register sorted by ROI. Auto-updatable weekly. Use when:
"what tech debt should we fix", "debt register", "list our HACKs", "what's slowing
us down".
tech-debt-register.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "tech debt register", "list tech debt", "what should we fix".

threat-hunt.routing:
Generates a hunt
hypothesis from a threat profile ("an attacker with foothold X would do
behavior Y to achieve objective Z"), translates it into queries against
declared log sources (CloudTrail / EDR / DNS / identity / process /
Splunk / Elastic / Sentinel / Loki), executes, scores confidence, declares
outcome (found / not found / inconclusive), and proposes detection-
engineering candidates for confirmed patterns. Refuses to run without an
`~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml` declaration.
Use when: "threat hunt", "hunt for X behavior", "did anyone do Y in our
logs", "hypothesis hunt".

threat-intel.lead: [Blue Team / Defensive] Threat intelligence ingestion + relevance filter.
threat-intel.routing:
Ingests selected feeds (MISP, OpenCTI, commercial vendors, OSINT),
filters indicators to Hall's stack (declared in security-ownership.yaml),
enriches with confidence + sightings, dedups, prioritizes by adversary
relevance. Output: daily/weekly briefing + a typed IoC store
(`intel/iocs.jsonl` per S15 schema) consumable by /detect-engineering,
/threat-hunt, /log-analyze, /forensics, /honeypot, and Family F /recon.
Refuses to run without `~/.gstack/projects/{slug}/security-ownership.yaml`.
Use when: "ingest threat intel", "MISP feed", "OpenCTI", "what new IoCs
matter to us", "daily intel briefing".

threat-model-evolve.lead: [CISO Compliance + IR] Living STRIDE threat model that updates on /ship.
threat-model-evolve.routing:
Maintains a canonical docs/threat-model.md (assets, actors, flows, threats
per STRIDE letter) plus a changelog of every diff that touched a trust
boundary, data flow, or external dependency. On /ship, diffs the architecture
against the last threat model, asks "did this PR introduce a new trust
boundary or data flow?" and prompts for an update. Maps to SOC 2 CC3.1-3.4
(risk assessment), ISO 27001 A.5.7 (threat intel) + A.8.27 (secure dev),
HIPAA §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) (risk analysis), PCI-DSS Req 12.3.1
(risk-assessment process).
Use when: "threat model", "STRIDE", "update threat model", "risk assessment",
"did this PR change our attack surface", "trust boundary".

transpose.routing:
Reads a BLUEPRINT.md
(from /blueprint) and walks line-by-line through what STAYS, what ADAPTS, and
what gets REPLACED when transposing the mechanism from its source domain to
your target domain. Names the proprietary slots, surfaces target-domain edge cases
the source didn't have, and emits TRANSPOSITION.md — a bridge plan downstream
/clone-and-twist consumes. Refuses to produce a transposition with a trivial
domain shift (i.e., a literal clone). Use when asked to "transpose this study",
"plan the bridge", "ASL to TSL", or "inference to fine-tuning".

tts-design.lead: [Speech & Voice AI] TTS architecture + training plan.
tts-design.routing:
Given target spec (languages,
voice count, latency budget, expression requirements, license posture, deployment
constraints), recommends the right backbone (VITS, Tacotron2, StyleTTS2, XTTSv2,
Bark, Tortoise, F5-TTS, Coqui) with rationale. Outputs an /autoplan-full-compatible
build plan with data-prep, training, eval, and deployment phases.
Use when: "design a TTS system", "which TTS model should I use", "TTS architecture",
"voice synthesis stack", "speech synthesis plan".
tts-design.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "design a tts", "tts architecture", "which tts model", "speech synthesis plan".

unfreeze.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories again.
unfreeze.routing:
Use when you want to widen edit scope without ending the session.
Use when asked to "unfreeze", "unlock edits", "remove freeze", or
"allow all edits".

ux-audit.lead: [UX Audit] Persona-driven UX workflow audit on a live site.
ux-audit.routing:
Defines personas, scripts their
end-to-end workflows, executes them in the browser, and reports where each persona
gets stuck, confused, or hits dead ends. Unlike /plan-ux-review (reviews a plan)
or /qa (finds bugs), this skill simulates real user journeys on a running app.
Use when asked to "ux audit", "workflow audit", "simulate user journeys",
"persona test", "walk through as a user", or "test the user experience".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready and the app has
multiple user roles or multi-step workflows.

ux-pipeline.routing:
Runs /office-hours →
/discover-personas → /product-ci → /ux-workflows → /design-consultation → /mockup →
/interactive-mockup with gap detection between every step and an iteration loop until
all invariants pass + no stakeholder feedback remains open. Detects warm-start state
(skips already-produced artifacts). Drift-propagates downstream when upstream tokens
change. Hands stakeholder a clickable mockup URL. Hard cap of 5 loops.
Use when asked to "ux pipeline", "auto ux pipeline", "full ux mockup", "stakeholder
mockup", "design and mockup everything", or "run the ux pipeline".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI/UX or after major scope expansion.

ux-workflows.lead: [UX Pipeline] Produce docs/WORKFLOWS.md — one section per persona, each with entry points, 3-7 core end-to-end workflows (action→screen→outcome tuples), cross-persona
ux-workflows.routing:
hand-offs,
edge/error workflows, and an explicit out-of-scope statement. Distinct from /define-workflows
(which produces audit-stage gap-analysis input); /ux-workflows is mockup-stage and granular
enough to drive /mockup screen generation. Layer-feeding skill in the v3.1 UX Pipeline.
Use when asked to "ux workflows", "define user journeys", "workflows per persona",
"what does each persona do", or "map out the flows".
Proactively suggest after /discover-personas, before /mockup or /design-consultation.

vendor-risk.lead: [CISO Compliance + IR] Third-party risk inventory + scoring.
vendor-risk.routing:
Discovers every
SaaS vendor and sub-processor by scanning SDK imports, .env keys, IaC, and
/cso outputs. Pulls each vendor's SOC 2 / ISO status, DPA terms, sub-processor
list. Classifies by data sensitivity × access scope. Flags vendors that need
DPA renewal, sub-processor disclosure to customers (GDPR Art. 28(2)),
BAA renewal (HIPAA §164.308(b)(1)), or service-provider contracts (CCPA §1798.140(ag)).
Use when: "vendor risk", "third-party audit", "sub-processor inventory",
"DPA review", "BAA renewal", "vendor scoring", "TPRM".

vendor-score.lead: [CTO Strategy] Deep single-vendor evaluation across 12 dimensions: security posture, SLA, pricing, API quality, DX, data portability, support, ecosystem, financial health, compliance, roadmap...
vendor-score.routing:
Pulls SOC 2 (if linked), reads API docs, tries
the SDK, scores each dimension 1-5, flags red lines (data residency, source escrow,
MFA support, breach history). Output: `decisions/vendors/{vendor-slug}.md` plus a row
added to `decisions/vendors/vendor-matrix.md`. Use when: "evaluate this vendor",
"deep dive on Stripe", "vendor scorecard", "due diligence".
vendor-score.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "vendor scorecard", "evaluate vendor", "vendor due diligence".

verify-loop.lead: [Build & Autonomy] Self-healing verification loop for gstack.
verify-loop.routing:
Runs /investigate-workflow logic on built features,
auto-fixes BROKEN items, presents INCOMPLETE items to user, re-verifies up to 3
iterations, and detects regressions (fix breaks something new -> STOP).
Two entry points: automatic via /autobuild Phase 2.5, or standalone via /verify-loop.
Use when asked to "verify the build", "check completeness", "verify-loop",
"self-heal", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest after /autobuild or after a large implementation session.

voice-clone.lead: [Speech & Voice AI] End-to-end voice cloning workflow with consent + watermark guardrails.
voice-clone.routing:
Picks the right tier (zero-shot XTTSv2 / Tortoise, few-shot StyleTTS2
fine-tune, full fine-tune VITS) per requirements. Generates data-prep + training +
inaudible-watermark + eval pipeline. Includes synthetic-voice attribution test that
proves the output is YOUR clone, not someone else's voice. Refuses to operate
without a signed CONSENT declaration.
Use when: "clone a voice", "voice cloning", "synthetic voice", "voice replica",
"build a voice double".
voice-clone.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "clone my voice", "voice clone", "synthetic voice", "build a voice".

voice-eval.lead: [Speech & Voice AI] Multi-metric TTS / voice-clone eval harness.
voice-eval.routing:
Scores synthesized
audio on MOS (human) + MOSNet (predicted), speaker similarity (ECAPA-TDNN cosine),
WER (Whisper back-transcribe), prosody distance (F0 + duration), and emotion-classifier
accuracy. Stores baselines in the shared eval store (S9) for regression detection.
Produces a per-task perceptual scorecard. Real eval beats "does it sound good?".
Use when: "evaluate TTS quality", "voice eval", "MOS score", "speaker similarity",
"voice regression test", "TTS scorecard".
voice-eval.voice_line: Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "voice eval", "score this tts", "voice quality test", "tts regression".

workspace-sync.lead: [Multi-Repo] Sync shared workspace configs (tsconfig, eslint, prettier, Renovate, GH Actions, .editorconfig, .nvmrc) across consuming repos via Renovate-style PRs. Pulls
workspace-sync.routing:
a canonical
template, diffs against each consumer, opens scoped sync PRs, gates breaking diffs through
AskUserQuestion.
Use when asked to "sync workspace configs", "propagate eslint", "shared config drift",
"renovate-style PRs", or "fix config drift across repos".
